name: Endpoint Validity appraisal for Tad Cardiovascular Endothelial Bp bundle
summary: Accepted. Endpoints are valid for a cardiovascular/endothelial baseline when framed as surrogate vascular, hemodynamic, microcirculation, PAH disease, and safety endpoints. The claim does not treat FMD, PWV, BP, biomarkers, or 6MWD in PAH as proof of MI, stroke, mortality, longevity, or general cardiovascular prevention in healthy men.

findings.claim_or_issue: FMD, PWV, BP, microcirculation, endothelial biomarkers, cIMT, PSV, and EPC outcomes are consistently framed as surrogate or physiology endpoints.
findings.why_it_matters: Surrogate movement is not equivalent to cardiovascular event prevention or longevity benefit.

findings.claim_or_issue: PHIRST 6MWD, clinical worsening, and PAH quality-of-life outcomes are presented as PAH-specific pulmonary vascular evidence.
findings.why_it_matters: These outcomes are clinically meaningful in PAH but not general cardiovascular-health endpoints for healthy users.
